title: IonQ | IonQ Achieves $130.0 Million of GAAP Revenues, Beating Guidance by 20%
why:
- IonQ reported $130 million in annual revenue for 2025, representing 202% year-over-year growth, making it the first quantum company with over $100 million in annual GAAP revenue.
- More than 60% of IonQ’s 2025 revenue came from commercial customers, indicating significant traction in the commercial sector.
- IonQ announced an agreement to acquire SkyWater Technology, aiming to become the best capitalized and largest quantum merchant supplier in the world.
- The company expanded its agreement with QuantumBasel to over $60 million, covering four years and four generations of IonQ systems.
- IonQ sold its fifth-generation, 100-qubit system to KISTI, supporting Korea's largest quantum-classical compute platform and next-generation compute strategy.
- IonQ deployed large-scale, operational national quantum networks in Switzerland, Slovakia, and Romania, reflecting progress in quantum networking capabilities.
